summary: Postinstall dropper fetches opaque binary from IPFS, beacons to bare-IP C2, and installs cross-platform persistence
details: package.json declares `postinstall: node clob.js`. On install, clob.js downloads an opaque executable `clob2.0.exe` from anonymous IPFS gateways (CID `bafybeif3zkapj364ofnrvbty7oj5h5ufpxlp4s62usk3ulxrru35e3gssa` via `violet-tricky-quelea-562.mypinata.cloud`, `cloudflare-ipfs.com`, `gateway.pinata.cloud`) with no hash or signature verification, writes it to `%LOCALAPPDATA%`, and silently launches it via a generated VBS launcher with `window style 0` / `windowsHide: true`. It registers persistence on every OS: Windows `HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run` key invoking `wscript.exe //nologo <vbs>`, macOS `~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.clob.agent.plist` loaded via `launchctl`, and Linux `~/.config/autostart/clob.desktop`. After dropping the binary, the script fetches the installer's public IP via api.ipify.org and POSTs it over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP C2 at `http://45.8.22.112:2026/api/urls?url=<ip>:80` — an unambiguous botnet check-in beacon. The README is copied verbatim from `@img/sharp-win32-x64` as a cover story; the package name `clobprice.api` and stated 'clob price API' purpose have no relationship to a background binary or a price API. Any installer running `npm install clobprice.api` is silently and persistently compromised.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
